company_type: Contract Research Organization (CRO) - Drug Development Services

supply_chain.dependencies: Limited international source of supply for large research models including non-human primates (NHPs). Cambodia previously supplied approximately 60% of NHPs to the United States. Single-source supplier dependencies for certain products. China previously supplied significant portion of large research models but subject to export restrictions since COVID-19 pandemic.
supply_chain.risks: Disruptions from colony health problems, export/import restrictions, embargoes, tariffs, inflation, international trade regulations, foreign government instability, severe weather, increased competition, disruptions to air travel, activist campaigns, commercial disputes, supplier insolvency, geopolitical disputes. Cambodia NHP supply chain constriction due to legal matters. Limited global supply of NHPs. Dependence on third-party suppliers for raw materials in CDMO operations.

regulatory_risks: U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) investigations into non-human primate imports from Cambodia
SEC Enforcement Division inquiry related to NHP sourcing and related disclosures
Voluntary suspension of Cambodia NHP shipments to U.S. pending additional procedures with USFWS
FDA Modernization Act 2.0 eliminating animal use in research may materially affect business
Potential changes to animal welfare regulations (AWA, PHS Policy, AAALAC standards)
CITES restrictions on NHP exportation from certain countries
Endangered Species Act listing risks for certain NHP species
GLP and cGMP compliance requirements and FDA inspections
Data integrity and electronic records compliance (FDA, EMA, OECD)
Environmental, health, safety and sustainability law compliance (OSHA, EPA, Nuclear Regulatory Agency, DOT)
International trade compliance (export controls, import requirements, trade regulations)
Anti-bribery laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act, OECD Convention)
Data privacy and security laws (EU GDPR, UK GDPR, China PIPL and DSL, U.S. state laws including CCPA/CPRA)
Healthcare reform and drug pricing legislation (Inflation Reduction Act provisions)
Tax law changes including global minimum taxation requirements and potential changes by current U.S. administration
Clinical holds by FDA or other regulators on client trials due to manufacturing compliance concerns
Product liability and quality control failures in CDMO operations

sanction_risks: Ongoing conflicts between Russia and Ukraine or Israel and Hamas could heighten geopolitical risks
US-China relationship potentially influencing sourcing patterns and tariff costs
Potential bar from engaging in business transactions with certain clients due to U.S. or foreign law changes
Exchange controls and legal restrictions on repatriation of funds
